mappings.rationale: Directly covers Hinduism including its history, beliefs, practices, philosophy, and institutions — the primary subject of section 823.A-C.
Covers Hindu philosophy and the integral relation of philosophy and religion in Hinduism, directly relevant to 823.B.3.
Directly covers Sikhism, one of the other religions of India, the specific subject of 823.D.2.

Gandhi's life and thought are deeply intertwined with modern Hindu reform and the ethical dimensions of Hinduism, relevant to 823.A.9 on the modern period and 823.C.2.f on ethical and nationalist movements.
The Quran provides context for understanding Islamic influences on Sikhism, relevant to 823.D.2.a.i on Islamic background of Sikhism.
The Buddha emerged from the Hindu heterodox period, relevant to 823.A.4 on challenges to Brahmanism by reformers and ascetic groups.
Buddhism arose as a heterodox challenge to Brahmanism in India, directly relevant to 823.A.4 on the heterodox period.
Covers modern India including the role of Hinduism, Sikhism, and other religions in contemporary Indian society, relevant to 823.A.9-10 on the modern period and Hinduism today.
Indian cinema frequently engages with Hindu mythology and religious themes, relevant to 823.C.4 on cultural expressions of Hindu values and ideas.
Covers philosophical questions about the divine that are central to Hindu philosophical traditions, relevant to 823.B.3 on Hindu philosophy.
Provides comparative context for understanding Hinduism and other religions of India within the broader study of religion.
Covers approaches to myth relevant to understanding Hindu mythology, its varieties, modes of representation, and themes addressed in 823.B.2.
Tibetan Buddhism incorporates Tantric elements shared with Hindu Tantrism, relevant to 823.C.2.d on Tantrism and its connections to Indian religious traditions.
Provides comparative context for understanding Eastern religious philosophy alongside Hindu philosophy discussed in 823.B.3.
Provides comparative context for understanding Hinduism's monotheistic and theistic dimensions relative to Abrahamic traditions, relevant to 823.C.1.a on views about God.
Covers mystical and spiritual traditions relevant to understanding Hindu mysticism and its goals and methods described in 823.B.4.
